events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need assess hazard: unprotected trench ≥5 ft with workers in it, commercial, in-progress. OSHA 1926 Subpart P requires protective systems for trenches 5 ft or deeper unless excavation is in stable rock. Workers in unprotected trench is high/critical hazard, cave-in. Decision fail. Risk critical. Work stage in-progress. Component conditions: non-compliant, installed-defective? Trench protection absent, so maybe non-compliant. Findings: workers in trench 5 ft or deeper without protective system; no sloping/shoring/shielding; potential cave-in. Actions: evacuate workers immediately; install protective system (sloping, benching, shoring, trench box) per competent person; keep workers out until protected; inspect by competent person; ensure egress within 25 ft; locate underground utilities; barricade. References: OSHA 1926.650-.652, 1926.652(a)(1). Need valid JSON only.

    "Workers are inside a trench 5 ft or deeper with no protective system in place.",
    "No sloping, benching, shoring, or trench box is provided to prevent cave-in.",
    "Condition violates OSHA excavation requirements for trenches 5 ft and deeper."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Evacuate all workers from the trench immediately.",
    "Prohibit entry until an OSHA-compliant protective system is installed.",
    "Have a competent person evaluate soil classification and select/install sloping, benching, shoring, or a trench shield.",
    "Provide safe access/egress within 25 ft of workers before re-entry.",
    "Inspect the excavation and protective system by a competent person before allowing work to resume.",
    "Install barricades, warning signs, and surface protection to prevent falls and unauthorized access."
  ],
  "rationale": "OSHA 1926 Subpart P requires a protective system for any trench 5 ft or deeper unless the excavation is entirely in stable rock. Workers in an unprotected trench of this depth are exposed to a high risk of cave-in, which is one of the leading causes of excavation fatalities. The observed condition is an imminent danger and must be corrected before work continues.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1926 Subpart P - Excavations",
    "29 CFR 1926.652(a)(1) - Protective systems required for excavations 5 feet or deeper"
  ]
